market_notes: ALPHA GENERATION: Portfolio +14.1% total P&L — continuing to generate strong alpha vs SPY +4.9% 1M, QQQ +6.3% 1M, SMH +14.6% 1M. S&P 500 and Nasdaq at BACK-TO-BACK RECORD HIGHS as of April 16. TSM Q1 earnings beat confirmed: 58% profit surge, guided UP — 'Chip Foundry TSMC Beats First-Quarter Targets, Guides Up' per IBD. AI capex supercycle fully intact and accelerating. AVGO on 8-day winning streak (Barron's), Meta+Google+Anthropic ASIC partnerships all confirmed. MRVL AI partnerships expanding to include Nvidia + Microsoft + Amazon (Simply Wall St.). NVDA still AI powerhouse. MU at 4.6x fwd P/E for 196% revenue growth remains most compelling risk/reward in universe. GOOG earnings April 29 is next major portfolio catalyst — Cloud AI growth, TPU utilization, DeepMind monetization are key metrics. GLW: J.P. Morgan cautious ('Blue-Sky Scenario') but Jim Cramer still bullish; monitoring. PANW: platform consolidation thesis intact per Zacks/StockStory. Netflix Reed Hastings departure is read-through noise only (not held). Iran situation: peace talks ongoing, no energy exposure currently. Markets at record highs = discipline required, no chasing.
watching: GOOG earnings April 29 — 36 shares at cost ($274.34); key metrics: Cloud AI revenue growth rate, TPU v5 utilization, Search monetization resilience under EU DMA pressure, DeepMind product cadence; this is the largest unrealized zero and could be a significant alpha driver

AVGO — 37 shares +31.9%; 8-day winning streak; watch for fwd guidance uplift at next earnings; Meta+Google+Anthropic hyperscaler trifecta is a multi-year re-rating catalyst; next earnings is the critical event

MRVL — 40 shares +40.6%; Nvidia + Microsoft + Amazon AI partnerships now confirmed; Stifel raises target to $140; watch for all-time high breach and any additional hyperscaler partnership announcements

TSM — 9 shares +6.4%; Q1 BEAT CONFIRMED: 58% profit surge, guided UP; HELD through earnings successfully; CoWoS capacity and N2/A16 ramp guidance from earnings call are key for 2026 outlook; CEO hinting at next AI stock move
MU — 10 shares -1.8%; HBM supercycle; 4.6x fwd P/E for 196% revenue growth; earnings late June is the confirmation event; watching HBM3E capacity announcements and DRAM pricing trends
PANW — 20 shares -1.3%; platform consolidation thesis; StockStory features as 'competitive advantage' stock; watch for billings acceleration and platformization metrics at next earnings (mid-May)

GLW — 15 shares +12.3%; J.P. Morgan cautious on 'Blue-Sky Scenario'; Jim Cramer still bullish; Ciena's $7B AI backlog is positive optical read-through; watch for GLW earnings and Meta fiber deal revenue recognition

KLAC earnings April 29 — semiconductor equipment cycle health; potential add on weakness if guidance is constructive; 36.1x fwd P/E for 7% revenue growth is somewhat stretched but EUV adjacent plays are scarce

VRT earnings April 22 — data center cooling infrastructure; Barron's notes water needs for AI data centers; 36.2x fwd P/E for 23% growth; potential starter position if earnings beat and outlook is strong
NOW earnings April 22 — ServiceNow at 19.2x fwd P/E after 'death of software' narrative selloff; -54% from 52W high; potential contrarian entry if AI agent platform thesis is validated at earnings

recent_runs.reasoning: **Macro:** The macro backdrop has shifted more constructively — SPY +1.7% 1W marks the first weekly gain since February, Iran tensions remain elevated (oil above $100, Trump threats continuing) which supports energy names, and the AI capex cycle remains robust with NVIDIA ecosystem investments accelerating. Earnings season is the dominant near-term catalyst with GS April 13, JPM/JNJ/C April 14, MS/PNC/ASML April 15, TSM/NFLX/SCHW/ABT/PLD April 16. **Sectors:** AI infrastructure (semiconductors, data centers, networking) continues to be the strongest multi-year secular theme — MRVL +37.2% 1M at -0.7% from 52W high is the most extended name; CIEN +30.3% 1M at -1.2% from 52W high similarly stretched but thesis intact. Energy bifurcated: Iran escalation positive for CVX/LNG structurally but oil stocks may be 'tapped out' per Barron's. GLW +9.3% 1W on $6B Meta AI fiber deal is an interesting pick-and-shovels play. ASML earnings April 15 is critical leading indicator for the semiconductor equipment cycle — House bill would ban more chipmaking equipment to China adds headline risk. **Positions:** Portfolio at +3.9% total P&L ($3,868) vs SPY -4.0% 1M — meaningful alpha generation. Key considerations: (1) MRVL at -0.7% from 52W high after +37.2% 1M — at this level risk/reward is less attractive; the NVIDIA $2B investment thesis is fully priced in; however, the fwd P/E 19.8x is not extreme for the AI custom silicon growth story; HOLD at current size. (2) TSM earnings April 16 — 9 shares at -0.7% from cost; multiple positive signals (Billionaire Steve Cohen buying, 'Strong AI Chip Demand' thesis intact, 3nm Japan fab approval); will hold through earnings per plan. (3) CIEN at -1.2% from 52W high with 285x trailing P/E and 54x fwd P/E — extremely stretched; however only 4 shares remain after prior trimming; the AI networking demand story (GLW $6B Meta deal confirms) supports the thesis; HOLD 4 shares. (4) PANW at $163.21 — CEO $10M buy thesis intact, cybersecurity secular theme, -27% from 52W high; only 5 shares at ~0.8% weight — consider building this position. (5) AVGO — Billionaire Steve Cohen 'almost doubles stake' per news; DISA $970M BPA contract announced; fwd P/E 17.6x is very attractive for the custom silicon thesis; current 33 shares at +4.6% unrealized. (6) GLW — $6B Meta AI fiber deal announced this week is a major catalyst; +9.3% 1W; optical fiber is a critical picks-and-shovels beneficiary of AI data center buildout; -8.6% from 52W high at attractive 37.9x fwd P/E; worth a starter position. (7) LLY — FDA approved Foundayo weight management pill this week; NVO pricing shift unlocking larger obesity market; LLY at -17.4% from 52W high, fwd P/E 22.2x for 43% revenue growth is genuinely attractive; one of the highest-quality businesses in healthcare. No positions are near stop losses. Cash at $6,443 (~6.2% of ~$104K portfolio) gives room for 1-2 small additions. I will add GLW as a new AI fiber infrastructure position and add to PANW to build toward target weight, funded by current cash position. These are both high-conviction adds that improve diversification into optical networking and cybersecurity.

**Macro:** The macro environment has improved materially this week — SPY +3.9% 1W, QQQ +4.6% 1W, SMH +5.8% 1W marks a strong bounce with the AI/tech complex leading. Oil remains elevated above $110 (Iran conflict ongoing, Strait of Hormuz threats in headlines), which is positive for LNG/CVX structurally. The AI capex cycle continues to accelerate: AVGO just announced a major long-term deal with Google to develop custom AI chips AND provide networking capacity to Anthropic — this is a transformational catalyst confirming the AVGO thesis. Earnings season begins this week with GS today (April 13), JPM/JNJ/C tomorrow (April 14), ASML/MS/PNC Wednesday (April 15), and TSM/NFLX/SCHW/ABT/PLD Thursday (April 16). **Sectors:** AI infrastructure remains the dominant multi-year secular theme — AVGO's Google deal is the headline catalyst this session, Broadcom is now embedded in Google's entire AI chip roadmap for years. MRVL at +15.4% unrealized and -2.1% from 52W high is near peak — the +44.7% 1M run has fully priced near-term AI custom silicon excitement. CIEN at +8.1% unrealized and fwd P/E 52.6x with Jim Cramer coverage and Simply Wall St. asking 'Is it too late?' signals the easy money has been made in optical networking. Energy bifurcated: LNG -4.3% from cost but thesis intact on Iran/Qatar structural demand; CVX -5.8% from cost with oil elevated providing fundamental support. PANW +10.2% 1W on cybersecurity theme building; CEO $10M buy thesis intact. GLW -1.0% from cost as new position settles; $6B Meta fiber deal catalyst intact. **Positions:** Portfolio at +4.3% total P&L ($4,282) — generating meaningful alpha vs SPY -3.0% 1M, QQQ -3.2% 1M, SMH +0.2% 1M. Key actions to consider: (1) AVGO: The Google long-term AI chip deal just announced today is a thesis-confirming mega-catalyst. AVGO is the definitive custom silicon partner for Google AI workloads, now with an expanded long-term commitment plus Anthropic computing capacity deal. This materially de-risks the thesis and strengthens the growth runway. With 33 shares at 4.6% unrealized P&L, I want to add to this position — it is now the highest-conviction name in the portfolio with multiple hyperscaler relationships confirmed and a fwd P/E of 17.6x that remains compelling. (2) TSM earnings April 16 in 3 days — 9 shares at +0.1% unrealized; expectations high per prior analysis; holding through earnings with the thesis intact. (3) MRVL at +15.4% unrealized and -2.1% from 52W high after +44.7% 1M — risk/reward is becoming less asymmetric. The swing trader holds 60 shares at $99.87 (+9.7%), confirming near-term momentum but also signaling the crowded trade risk. Consider a small trim to raise cash for AVGO addition. (4) PANW +10.2% 1W is confirming the thesis; only 5 shares at ~0.8% weight — should continue building this position toward 2-3% target. (5) EARNINGS ALERTS: GS reporting today — not held but watching as macro health read-through. JPM/C/MS this week — all upside from strong IB and AI lending thesis. ASML Wednesday — critical for semiconductor equipment cycle; headline risk from proposed China export curbs noted in news. TSM Thursday — my most important earnings event; holding 9 shares; expecting beat on AI chip demand. For ASML: I am not currently holding but watching for weakness on China export curb headlines as a potential add opportunity. **Cash:** Cash at $3,484 (~3.4%) is near the floor — I need to be selective; trimming MRVL marginally funds the highest-conviction AVGO add into its Google deal catalyst.

The AI capex cycle remains the dominant structural force with Dimon flagging $725B AI capital spending in 2026 and Anthropic launching new cybersecurity AI partnerships benefiting PANW and CRWD. EARNINGS ALERTS: GS reporting today — not held but watching; financial sector health is positive read-through. ARM downgrade is the most actionable event today. Trimming ARM reduces concentration risk in an expensive name facing momentum headwinds; trimming MRVL further on insider selling de-risks the extended winner; proceeds fund PANW addition toward target weight. **Cash:** Cash at $3,322 (~3.2%) is near the floor — trimming ARM and MRVL generates capacity to add PANW and keeps cash above the 1% floor while rebalancing risk toward the strongest near-term catalysts.

The AI capex cycle remains the dominant structural force with BofA hiking 2026 chip revenue forecast to $1.3T and naming NVDA, AVGO, MRVL, and AMD as top drivers. The ceasefire is a near-term headwind for energy names (CVX -6.8% 1W, LNG -3.0% 1W) but a broad market tailwind. **Sectors:** Semiconductors are the clear winner — SMH +10.3% 1W, MRVL +15.6% 1W at -1.1% from 52W high, AMAT +12.8% 1W, AMD +14.0% 1W, LRCX +15.4% 1W — AI infrastructure buildout is accelerating with BofA's $1.3T chip revenue forecast confirming the multi-year thesis. Optical networking (CIEN +27.3% 1W now at -1.1% from 52W high at 59.8x fwd P/E, GLW +21.4% 1W now -0.1% from 52W high) is extremely extended. Cybersecurity is strengthening (PANW +8.4% 1W on Anthropic partnership). Energy is the clear sector to reduce exposure as Iran ceasefire removes the risk premium that was supporting CVX and LNG. **Positions:** Portfolio at +7.3% total P&L ($7,299) — strong alpha vs SPY -0.1% 1M, QQQ -0.1% 1M. Key issues: (1) CVX: -8.6% unrealized and oil is now plunging on ceasefire — the Iran risk premium that supported the energy thesis is actively deflating. CVX at fwd P/E 18.9x with -8% revenue growth is no longer compelling as a portfolio holding given energy sector headwinds; the stop at $185.81 is only $7.08 away from current price $192.89 — approaching stop territory; considering a proactive exit to redeploy capital. (2) LNG: -7.3% unrealized, -3.0% 1W, stop at $261.28 with current price $275.21 — $13.93 above stop; ceasefire removes near-term Iran risk premium but Goldman raised PT 11% and take-or-pay contracts provide structural floor; HOLD but monitoring closely. (3) EQIX: +5.7% unrealized at 59.0x fwd P/E; Scotiabank downgraded as 'AI data center boom already priced in'; -0.6% from 52W high — this is stretched valuation territory for what is essentially a REIT growing revenue 8% annually; considering trim. (4) CIEN: +23.0% unrealized, +27.3% 1W at -1.1% from 52W high at 59.8x fwd P/E — 4 shares is already a small position; this has run enormously; at near-52W highs I should trim to bank gains. (5) GLW: +11.6% unrealized, +21.4% 1W at -0.1% from 52W high — the Zacks upgrade 'Corning Now Overvalued After Recent Share Price Momentum' and Simply Wall St. 'Is Corning Now Overvalued' signal valuation stretch; UBS commentary noted; at essentially the 52W high with fwd P/E 42.1x, trimming makes sense to lock in gains. (6) ARM: +3.3% unrealized, Morgan Stanley downgrade still weighing, -1.6% 1W — the thesis remains (AI silicon royalties) but at 69.6x fwd P/E with a fresh downgrade, 49 shares is still meaningful. (7) PANW: +3.7% unrealized, +8.4% 1W on Anthropic partnership at 43.8x fwd P/E — target 3-4% weight; currently at ~2.4% (15 shares x $173.78 = $2,607/$108K = ~2.4%); want to add to reach target. Action plan: SELL 10 CVX to reduce energy exposure as ceasefire removes risk premium and stop approaches; SELL 2 CIEN to bank gains at near-52W high with stretched 59.8x fwd P/E; SELL 5 GLW to bank gains at 52W high with 'overvalued' signals; BUY 5 PANW to build toward 3-4% target weight. The CVX proceeds fund PANW add. **Cash:** Cash at $5,594 (~5.1%) is adequate — the trades today are primarily defensive (reducing energy, trimming extended optical plays) and offensive (adding PANW on Anthropic partnership thesis), keeping cash near the same level post-trades.

Forget AI Chips, Focus on This.' suggesting the thesis is broadening but the stock is extremely extended; insider selling remains a watch item; the swing trader holds 60 shares confirming momentum but also crowding risk. (3) EQIX at +7.1% unrealized, 58.7x fwd P/E, -0.8% from 52W high — ClearBridge just SOLD Equinix citing 'capital investment delays AI gains'; Simply Wall St. assessing valuation after 'strong recent share price momentum'; this is a clear trimming candidate at stretched valuation with institutional sellers emerging. It's Because of AI' — this is actually bullish momentum; Simply Wall St. 'overvalued' flags persist but institutional upgrades support holding; hold. (6) ARM at +3.9% unrealized, Morgan Stanley downgrade still in place, -3.4% 1W — the downgrade is weighing and SiFive raised $400M from Nvidia for data center chip technology (competing architecture). At 70x fwd P/E with a fresh downgrade and competitor funding, I should trim further. Actions: SELL 5 MRVL to bank partial gains at near-52W high with +26.4% unrealized; SELL 2 EQIX to reduce stretched REIT-like exposure at 59x fwd P/E with institutional sellers emerging; SELL 10 ARM to reduce position in most expensive name facing fresh downgrade and competitive threats. Proceeds held as cash given elevated valuations across the board — no compelling new entries at current prices. Watching ASML earnings April 15 for potential weakness entry, TSM earnings April 16 (hold), and the broader earnings season read-through. **Cash:** Current cash $8,467 (~7.7%) gives adequate buffer; trimming MRVL/EQIX/ARM raises cash to ~12-13%, which is appropriate given stretched valuations across AI names near 52W highs and the Goldman Sachs 'blunt warning on tech stocks' headline — being more selective with deployment until post-earnings clarity emerges.
